compiled_state.title: Bash-to-Python strangler-fig migration (Tiers A-E, kill-switch per tier)
compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why
Rec 1 + Rec 9 of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risks R3/R4/R5/R8/R9). 14.9k LOC of bash with ~140 embedded python3 heredocs (27 in ticket-lib-api.sh, 20 in ticket-lib.sh) is the dominant maintenance liability for agent-driven development. The hard-won lessons live in the I1-I9 doctrine, the already-Python critical sections (ticket_txn.py, reducer, graph), and the 1,400+ test corpus — not in the bash itself. The regression-safe playbook is already proven in-repo: REBAR_NATIVE_READS (commits 7d53bc5 -> a93885e): port behind a kill-switch, pin parity, soak, remove the switch.

## What
Tiered strangler-fig port; each tier spawns its own child tickets when started; the relevant bash suite is the per-command characterization gate, run against BOTH impls until byte-identical before any default flips:
- Tier A: read shims (show/list/ready/search/deps) -> dispatcher calls _reads.py (story rich-glare-sake).
- Tier B: leaf writes (comment, tag/untag, set-file-impact, set-verify-commands, archive, scratch) -> rebar._commands; may temporarily shell to the bash write core.
- Tier C: compute-heavy bash (ticket-next-batch.sh 944 LOC, validate-issues.sh 945, ticket-list-epics.sh 449).
- Tier D: the write/sync core (_flock_stage_commit, _push_tickets_branch, _reconverge_tickets) LAST, behind REBAR_WRITE_CORE=bash|python, soaked one full release on this repo's own store.
- Tier E: delete the dispatcher; cli.py becomes a real argparse CLI over the same _commands functions as the library. Unpacked-disk constraint, jq prerequisite, and flock fallback all retire here.
Test consolidation rides along (Rec 9): each ported command's bash suite is translated to pytest and retired only after the default flips and soaks; parity tests are deleted as each duplication collapses.

## Scope
The full bash engine over multiple releases; one tier (and within tiers, one command) at a time. NOT in scope: any change to the store/locking design (ratified sound) or the event schema.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tier ordering + kill-switch discipline documented in docs/architecture.md and followed (default stays bash until parity green + dogfood soak)
- [ ] Each port ticket carries its bash suite as the characterization gate (byte-identical before flip)
- [ ] End state: one implementation + three facades; zero standalone shell test harness; no embedded heredocs

comments.body: Detailed implementation plan committed at docs/bash-migration.md (73be64b, task prim-myth-grain): tier B-E scope tables, per-tier kill-switches (REBAR_LEAF_WRITES/REBAR_COMPUTE/REBAR_WRITE_CORE) with the REBAR_PUSH parsing idiom, the 7-step per-command protocol generalized from REBAR_NATIVE_READS (7d53bc5b->a93885e), the Tier B event-append seam preserving I5, the Tier D dual-window lock-interop rule (fcntl+mkdir both-legs acquisition), and the concurrency validation matrix (mixed-impl same-clone storms, two-clone cross-core convergence, mixed-version fleets). docs/architecture.md module-size section now anchors AC #1 to it. Planning surfaced bug stiff-mop-lane (bash mkdir-fallback writes don't contend with ticket_txn.py fcntl.flock on flock(1)-less platforms) — child of this story, fix due no later than Tier D.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Tier B shipped in nava-rebar 0.5.0 across all 3 channels (PyPI / Homebrew tap / MCP Registry). This comment was written by the PUBLISHED 0.5.0 build through the now-default python leaf-write path against the live store.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Tier E IN PROGRESS (2026-06-13, pushed origin/main @ 3bdf1897). Kickoff lad-cipher-arena (4387-8fae-9c8b-444b). DONE: E0 argparse CLI cutover (cli.py now = rebar._cli, byte-parity help/overview/error via package-data goldens; auto-init/_ensure_initialized middleware ported), E1 entrypoint cutover, E2 all read-ish/quality commands in-process (field-reads, exists/resolve/format, list-descendants, clarity/check-ac/quality/summary) with dual-run parity + library rewired off _run. Pre-existing CI failures fixed (REPO_ROOT leak + macOS tmp-race). 7 commits, full suite 1660 passed. REMAINING: E3 write/lifecycle (+E5c ticket_txn relocation), E4 init/fsck, E5/E5b bridge+reconciler (live-DIG Jira gate), E6 shims, E7 dispatcher delete, E8 suites->pytest, E9 close. Detailed handoff: docs/tier-e-execution-plan.md.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body: Tier E progress (2026-06-14): E0–E4 DONE (reads, leaf writes, write/lifecycle trio, compact, scratch, delete, fsck, fsck-recover, init all in-process; auto-init consent gate added). E5–E9 remain (bridge cmds + reconciler rewire w/ LIVE-DIG Jira gate, shim deletion, dispatcher deletion, .sh->pytest, docs+close). PR #4 (env-bound manifest signing) merged into main @ eda41cc7 — SIGNATURE event + signature close-gate now live; integration verified. Full handoff on kickoff child lad-cipher-arena.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.body:
=== MIGRATION COMPLETE ===
Bash->Python strangler-fig migration done across all tiers (A-E). The story's end-state AC is met: ONE implementation + three facades (lib/CLI/MCP); zero _engine/*.sh; no embedded heredocs; zero standalone shell tests. The bash dispatcher + 36 .sh + helpers + shims + ticket_txn are deleted (commit e3507895); the reconciler + Jira probe + wordlist are the only engine package-data left, launched as subprocesses. Verified: full suite 1675 passed/0 failed; integration 8 passed; live DIG reconcile dry-run + bridge-probe green; all 3 interfaces dogfooded. Closing.
comments.author: Joe Oakhart
